symptoms.symptom_name: Profound social isolation and loneliness for six months

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute psychosomatic crisis secondary to social isolation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic isolation is the driver of the panic, self-neglect and hopelessness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Crushing chest tightness during panic — non-exertional, non-radiating, no diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic-associated chest tightness that settles with reassurance; non-exertional and non-radiating.
In a frail hypertensive elder, chest tightness cannot be attributed to anxiety without ECG/troponin — a telehealth exclusion gap.

symptoms.symptom_name: 48h refusal of all food and fluid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Two days of no intake in a frail elder is sufficient to cause clinically significant dehydration.
Refusal driven by apathy and distress rather than a primary GI cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss and progressive self-neglect

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Social isolation with self-neglect and malnutrition risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Months of poor nutrition and failing self-care reflect the underlying isolation syndrome.
Passive death wish in an isolated self-neglecting elder mandates an active suicidal-ideation screen and mental-health referral.

symptoms.symptom_name: Antihypertensive and statin withheld for over a week

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt cessation plus dehydration destabilises BP control; relevant to management and to the ACS consideration.
Clinician-measured tachycardia consistent with anxiety and mild volume depletion — the AI cannot measure heart rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor skin turgor and dry oral mucosa (clinician exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms mild clinical dehydration — obtainable only by in-person examination, sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alert, fully oriented, intact attention, no focal neurological deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Full orientation with intact attention on formal assessment argues strongly against delirium and acute stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal baseline ECG and U&E/renal bloods

investigation_details.expected_result: ECG without acute ischaemic change; bloods to quantify dehydration/AKI
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cardiac and renal causes remain unexcluded — the reason a telehealth AI must route to in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome / acute kidney injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive exclusion requires ECG/troponin and renal bloods — never available in real time over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, cough or urinary symptoms (afebrile on clinician measurement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever or urinary symptoms lowers (but does not fully exclude) an occult infection as the driver.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude an acute cardiac cause for the chest tightness in a frail hypertensive elder — telehealth cannot obtain it.
To quantify dehydration and detect acute kidney injury after 48h of no intake with a withheld antihypertensive.
Skin turgor, mucous membranes, auscultation and BP/HR cannot be assessed over chat.
Isolated, self-neglecting elder with a passive death wish requires a formal mental-health risk assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance dispatch for the baseline presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reported vitals are stable and there is no patient-reportable time-critical feature; 000 is reserved for the escalation triggers, not the baseline.
